name: Pheasant with Champagne Cabbage i (Pheasant)
recipeInstructions:
1. Carefully remove the breast halves from the pheasants and set aside.
2. Remove the legs with thighs attached.
3. To bone each leg-thigh portion, use a sharp boning or paring knife to slit the meat parallel to the bones. Carefully scrape thigh meat away from the bone.
4. Cut through the joint between the leg and thigh, keeping the meat in one piece. Scrape away all remaining thigh meat and cut out the thighbone.
5. Use a cleaver to cut off the knob end of the leg. Scrape meat from the knob and pull out the bone.
6. Refrigerate the meat, covered. Reserve bones and carcasses for Pheasant Stock.
Source: New York's Master Chefs, Bon Appetit Magazine
Written by Richard Sax, Photographs by Nancy McFarland
The Knapp Press, Los Angeles, 1985
Chef: Andy Kisler, Vienna 79 Restaurant, New York
